name: OpenClaw Monitor
description: Real-time monitoring console: aggregates diagnostic events, exposes overview / events / runs / logs REST endpoints, streams live events over SSE, and serves a self-contained dashboard at /monitor.

maxPerType.description: Maximum events retained in memory per event type (hot path).

kind.description: Persistence mode. `memory` disables on-disk storage entirely.

path.description: Override the storage directory. Defaults to ${stateDir}/openclaw-monitor.

audit.description: Conversation content audit. Captures the four touchpoints (project → OpenClaw, OpenClaw → LLM, LLM → OpenClaw, OpenClaw → project) via plugin hooks. Enabled by default in v0.4.0+ — set audit.enabled=false to opt out.

enabled.description: When true, captures full prompt and response text. Storage grows with conversation volume. Set false to opt out.

contentMaxBytes.description: Truncate any single content string longer than this. Default raised to 1 MB in v0.6.0 so the conversation detail view shows full prompts and responses without truncation; bump up to 16 MB for extreme outliers. Truncated segments are flagged with truncated=true.

retainDays.description: Daily JSONL files older than this are deleted. Keep this short — content files grow much faster than event files.

captureSystemPrompt.description: When true (default in v0.4.0+), the system prompt is included in llm_input segments. Set false to redact it (some deployments treat system prompts as commercial secrets).

autoTrustHostGates.description: When true (default in v0.5.0+), the plugin auto-writes plugins.allow and plugins.entries.openclaw-monitor.hooks.allowConversationAccess on first start so audit works with zero manual setup. Set false if you want explicit operator opt-in via `openclaw monitor setup --audit`. Auto-write logs a loud warn and requires a gateway restart to take effect.

alerts.description: Threshold-based alert engine (v0.7.0+). Evaluates rule conditions against the rolling-window metrics every `evaluationIntervalSec` and pushes notifications to configured channels (generic webhook / DingTalk custom robot). Disabled by default — set alerts.enabled=true and add at least one channel + one rule to activate.

enabled.description: Master switch. When false the engine doesn't start regardless of the rules array.

evaluationIntervalSec.description: How often (in seconds) the engine compares each rule's metric to its threshold. Lower = faster reaction but more CPU; 30s is fine for most operational SLOs.

channels.description: Notification destinations keyed by an arbitrary channel id. Rules reference these ids in their `channels: []` array. At least one channel must be defined or rules silently fail their delivery step.

kind.description: `webhook` POSTs a generic JSON payload (AlertNotification shape). `dingtalk` posts a markdown card to a DingTalk custom robot.

url.description: Full destination URL. For DingTalk include the `access_token` query parameter.

headers.description: Optional extra HTTP headers (webhook channel only — DingTalk uses fixed JSON content-type).

secret.description: DingTalk only. Optional HMAC-SHA256 signing secret; when present, every request is signed with timestamp + sign query parameters per DingTalk custom-robot security rules.

atMobiles.description: DingTalk only. Phone numbers to @-mention in the alert message.

atAll.description: DingTalk only. Set true to @everyone in the target group.

rules.description: Alert rule list. Each rule watches one rolling-window metric and fires when the comparison `metric op threshold` is true for a single evaluation cycle.

id.description: Stable identifier (a-z, 0-9, -). Used to correlate firing/resolution state across restarts.

metric.description: Which WindowSnapshot field to compare. See src/types.ts: WindowSnapshot.

window.description: Which rolling window to read the metric from.

cooldownSec.description: Minimum seconds between repeated firing notifications. The engine keeps re-evaluating during this window but suppresses notifications.

channels.description: Channel ids (keys of alerts.channels) to notify on fire / resolve.

pricing.description: Per-1000-token price table used by the cost engine (v0.8.0+). Tokens are always counted regardless of the table; a model that is not listed contributes zero cost (no error). All prices share a single currency — no FX conversion happens.

currency.description: Display unit for every cost figure. Free-form (e.g. CNY, USD, EUR). Make sure every entry in `models` uses the same currency.

models.description: Price entries keyed by the canonical provider/model reference (the same string the Models page shows). Each entry sets per-1000-token rates.

cacheRead.description: Optional. Cost per 1,000 cache-read tokens (Anthropic-style prompt caching). Defaults to `input` when omitted.

cacheWrite.description: Optional. Cost per 1,000 cache-write tokens. Defaults to `input` when omitted.
